title: Death and Rebirth and my mother
display_title: Death and Rebirth and my mother
wikitext:
[]: Death & Rebirth... and my mother.             Quote Post by Omegagouki » Sun Feb 25, 2007 7:39 am Sooo... I got Death & Rebirth from Netflix on Tuesday. It is a lovely recap and character study, and a preview to the End. Not all too interesting to talk about, right? Well. The Netflix account is moreover, my mother's; we just share a queue. So, she wanted to watch it after we got it, out of curiosity, I suppose. Well, I watched it a few times by myself, before she had the time one night to watch it. When she asked if it was good, I said "yes". Frankly, I was nervous, as one should be about having a parental watch something such as this. So, when the time came that she wanted to watch it... I chickened out and went to bed. But, I know she watched it, as I heard some of it from my room before I fell asleep (Shinji, you've got a fantastic set of lungs, boy). However, the next day, even though I know she watched it, she hasn't mentioned anything about D&R to me, no opinions, no complaints, nothing. So, I guess my nervousness about it was silly, but... Now I'm nervous of her not saying anything. And I haven't had the courage to ask what she thought, either. So, what now? I still have it here, as I'm off and on working on transcribing the... you-know-what ...so, shall I ask her what she thought and/or watch it with her this time, or not ever bother with it again, until EoE comes in the mail and it shatters her mind is she gets curious and watches it alone like she did, here? Oh, and you can also blather about D&R related stuff if you like.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sun Feb 25, 2007 9:26 am She prolly just thought it was stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Feb 25, 2007 9:42 am D&R makes little sense if you aren't familiar with NGE to begin with. After my raves about NGE, I actually got my parents to watch the entire series. Neither are anime fans, though they like the occasional movie and rare series. My mom loved Spirited Away and Grave of the Fireflies, dad loved Princess Mononoke and Ghost in the Shell, both loved Perfect Blue and Escaflowne. After watching it, my mom said she liked it but didn't understand much of it (cue me trying to explain all of NGE to her. :shock:), and my dad loved it. His words after watching EoE were "that was pretty mind boggling." But he also loved the Matrix so I figured this would be right up his alley. I'd just talk about it with her and tell her about the series, characters, plot, and its intrinsic depth and complexity. I'd say "it's an excellent series if you're willing to put in the time to actively watch the entire series and the movie." All great art takes time and effort to get something out of it. It's like anything else, the more you put in, the more you get out. Music, movies, art, anime, literature, it's all the same. Most casual fans of "entertainment" don't like shows like NGE because they have the attention span of a fruit fly and their eye for depth and complexity goes about as far as "ooh, look at the big explosion." [/rant] Personally, I would've told my mom about D&R and NGE before I had her watch it. D&R is a nice little recap, but the series has too much going on up to EoE to sum it up all nice and neatly in 1 hour (the 24 episodes up to that are 8+ hours long by my count). NGE, including the entire series + EoE is about 10.5 hours long I think. About 20.5 minutes an episode (not counting song, end credits and previews), multiplied by 26 episodes, add about 10/15 minutes for the extended "director's cut" episodes, + 90 minutes for EoE, divided by 60 = 10.55. If you're sane, it can be watched in 3 days if you devote enough time to it. 4 days would be a nice 2.5 hours a day.

[]:             Quote Post by C4 » Sun Feb 25, 2007 11:44 am My second time watching the series, I watched it with my grandfather.(And "Cruel Angel's Thesis is is favorite song hands down now.) He enjoyed it greatly, though since he's not the type to analyze anything, he didn't even want to try to get into the heavy stuff. Despite the fact that he loved the series, I was real nervous about showing him EoE. He's a pretty hardcore Christian, and whenever there's a nude scene in a movie, he almost always immediately turns it off.(Violence is a non-factor though; that's American culture for ya.) I decided to show him the movie, and I was really nervous about the hospital scene. I paused right after it was over and the conversation went something like: Me: So, you know what just happened, right? Grandpa: No, what just happened? Me: Well, Shinji just sorta... well, you know. Grandpa: ...? Me: You know? Grandpa: ... Wait, so you mean that- Me: Yeah, he did something pretty bad. Grandpa: You mean he didn't wash his hands afterwards? He got through it alright, and after it was all over, the first words that came out of his mouth were, "What the hell was that?" He liked it a lot, but he thought it was really depressing, and he liked EoTV better. Yeah, and D&R should never be shown to anyone who isn't familiar with Eva unless you're there to sort of tell them what's happening. I've shown D&R/EoE to at least three of my friends, and they all said that they would have had no clue what was happening unless I had told them. If your ma hadn't seen anything before D&R, she probably had no idea what was happening. And when you have no idea what's happening in a movie, you're bound to give reviews like this. [url]http://goforit.comicgen.com/rants/evangelion.html[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Feb 25, 2007 11:59 am Lol, I love reading reviews like that. Sometimes I wonder if the reviewer is actually that retarted or if they have to really try to be. Either way, that's quite hilarious. I did the same thing with my mom during the hospital scene (yeah, that's not uncomfortable at all). Me: You know what just happened right? Her: No, what? Me: You can't figure it out? Her: No, rewind it. Me: *Rewind* Notice Asuka... and Shinji.... with the heavy breathing, locked door, Shinji's hand.... Her: ???? Me: Nevermind :oops: I think she got it, but was trying to spare discussing it with me, the saintly woman.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Feb 25, 2007 12:14 pm Why are you people asking your parents if they understood the masturbation scene? You are automatically asking for an embarrassing/uncomfortable situation. They're adults, I'm sure they understood what happened, and don't mention it because they're just as apprehensive to talk about it as you are.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Feb 25, 2007 7:51 pm I just finished running my grandmother through an NGE marathon (skipping D&R owing to time constraints). She liked it. I had to stop and explain a few things, mostly technical or cultural background stuff, but she really got into the characters. My parents, meanwhile, still maintain their snobbish standoff.

[]:             Quote Post by Houndour » Sun Feb 25, 2007 10:50 pm Heh, showing it to grandparents? If mine were still around I might have showed some of it them, though I did try to teach Grandma how to play the Pokemon tcg years ago. :P For some reason I have the feeling they wouldn't be interested, anyway. I watched EoE with one of my brothers today, that turned out better than I thought it would. Though, I did brief him for about half an hour with my limited and sloppy knowledge about NGE. He had seen about 10 episodes beforehand as well, so that helps.
